new_classes.label_en: PDO wine (white), in containers > 2 l and ≤ 10 l (CN 2204 22 26)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit g.U. (Weißwein), in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 l bis einschließlich 10 l (KN 2204 22 26)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes with a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO / g.U.), white wine, presented in containers of more than 2 litres and up to and including 10 litres, classified under CN code 2204 22 26.

new_classes.label_en: PDO wine production process (CN 2204 22, containers > 2 l and ≤ 10 l)
new_classes.label_de: Herstellungsprozess für Wein mit g.U. (KN 2204 22, Behältnisse > 2 l bis 10 l)
new_classes.definition_en: Production process for still wine of fresh grapes that is assigned a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO/g.U.), occurring within a designated PDO wine region, and resulting in wine presented in medium containers (> 2 l and ≤ 10 l).
A quality borne by a wine indicating that it is white (as opposed to red or rosé), used to discriminate between CN subheadings for PDO still wines in containers > 2 l and ≤ 10 l.

new_classes.label_en: Protected Designation of Origin (PDO/g.U.) wine quality
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a wine that has been assigned a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO / g.U.) as defined in EU wine regulations and referenced by Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 of the CN.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the four key discriminating criteria for CN 2204 22 26: (1) wine of fresh grapes (parent class WineFreshGrapesGrapeMust2204), (2) PDO/g.U. designation (PDOWineQuality + PDOWineProductionProcess22042200 occurring in PDOWineRegionSite), (3) white wine colour (WhiteWineColourQuality, discriminating from the red/rosé sibling 22042224), (4) medium container size > 2 l and ≤ 10 l (MediumContainer2To10L + numeric range on liquidVolumeInLitres), and (5) not sparkling / not semi-sparkling (complement restrictions on SparklingWineQuality and SemiSparklingWineQuality). Coverage is reduced to 0.85 because the legal text references Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 (defining the precise PDO criteria, including reference to EU Regulation 1308/2013 and related delegated acts), which cannot be fully formalised without the normative text of that Additional Note being available in the ontology context. The specific wine colour threshold distinguishing 'white' from other colours (red/rosé) is modelled via a new quality class but the precise regulatory colour definition is not further axiomatised.
